key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 basic EPS HK$14.05 normalized down for peak turnover / margin-fund pass-through (below spot EPS)
Peak activity FY2024–FY2025; base owner cash below reported EPS HK$14.05

open_questions: [ ] Confirm live price in HKD before sizing

[ ] No third-party sources in base IRR (inventory empty)
thesis: Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing (HKEX) operates the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, derivatives and commodities markets (including London Metal Exchange ownership), and central clearing and settlement infrastructure. **[Fact]** Brokers, issuers, and clearing members pay trading, clearing, listing, data, and depository fees; the group does not run a balance-sheet lending franchise on core exchange operations. **[Fact]** FY2024 revenue and other income was **HK$22.4 billion** (+9% year-over-year) with profit attributable to shareholders **HK$13.1 billion** (+10%) and basic earnings per share **HK$10.32** (`0388.HK/official-reports/annual-reports/annual_report_fy2024.pdf`). HKEX is the dominant listing and trading gateway between international capital and Chinese issuers, with Connect programs linking Hong Kong to Shanghai and Shenzhen.
why_market_wrong: No dated mispricing edge at today's price. **[Inference]** FY2024–FY2025 benefited from strong headline average daily turnover (ADT **HK$131.8 billion** in FY2024, +26% year-over-year) and robust derivatives activity; Q1 2026 revenue **HK$8.2 billion** (+20% versus Q1 2025 per IR pack). **[Inference]** The market may be fairly valuing a peak-to-mid-cycle earnings stream at roughly **26 times** trailing earnings rather than underwriting permanent boom-level volumes. Return is franchise quality at price, not a catalyst stack.
